name: pm_project_remove
description: Remove a project and all its epics and stories from the project management system. This is a destructive operation — use only when a project is no longer needed. Always confirm with the user before calling this tool. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_epic_add
description: File a new epic to the project management system. Use this when decomposing a large goal into trackable work (new feature, major refactor, multi-part initiative) or when you discover a significant area of work that should be tracked. An epic is a theme with multiple independent stories — create the epic first, then file stories under it. Do NOT use this for small fixes — use story_add instead. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

project.description: Project code that owns the epic

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_story_add
description: File a new story to the project management system. Use this to break down work into independently completable tasks that can be executed by you or picked up by parallel agents. Also use this when you discover a specific, actionable piece of work (bug, improvement, tech debt) while working on something else. Write clear acceptance criteria so any agent can verify completion. The story will be added to an existing epic's backlog. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

epic.description: Epic code that will own the new story

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_status
description: Show current project management status. Use this to understand what projects exist, what work is in progress, and what's in the backlog before filing new items or picking up work. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

project.description: Optional project code override for MCP projection

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_gc_run
description: Run garbage collection on the local .pm/ directory to clean up stale artifacts — expired comments, consolidated reports, and superseded ADRs. Use this periodically to keep the project data lean. Supports a dry-run mode to preview what would be collected without making changes. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_adr_create
description: Create a new Architecture Decision Record (ADR). Use this to document architectural decisions with context, decision rationale, and consequences. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

status.description: ADR lifecycle status: proposed | accepted | deprecated | superseded

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_adr_query
description: Query Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) with filters and relevance ranking. Results are scored by tag match count plus recency and returned sorted by relevance. Use this to find relevant architectural decisions before making new ones, or to check existing guidance on a topic. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

status.description: ADR status filter: proposed | accepted | deprecated | superseded

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_comment_add
description: Add a comment to a target task for async cross-task communication. Use this to leave notes for other agents or humans working on related tasks. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_comment_list
description: List comments with optional filters. Use this to retrieve comments for a specific task or filtered by type/author. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_report_create
description: Create an execution report for a completed task. The report captures decisions, assumptions, tradeoffs, and observations to support the consolidation agent's work. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_report_view
description: View an execution report by task ID. Displays the report in human-readable format with section headers. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_agent_heartbeat
description: Send an agent heartbeat, creating or updating the agent's state file at .pm/agents/{agent_id}.yaml. Use this periodically during long-running tasks to signal that the agent is still alive and to record progress. The tool sets last_heartbeat to the current timestamp and preserves all other existing fields. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

status.description: Agent status: active | idle | needs_attention | blocked | completed (optional)

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_agent_escalate
description: Escalate an issue from an agent, setting its status to needs_attention and recording escalation details in .pm/agents/{agent_id}.yaml. Use this when the agent encounters a situation requiring human or supervisor intervention — a decision that needs approval, a clarification question, or an error that cannot be resolved autonomously. If the agent state file does not exist, it is created with started_at set to now. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

type.description: Escalation type: decision | clarification | approval | error

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_agent_check_response
description: Check for a human response to a previously escalated issue. Looks for .pm/agents/{agent_id}-response.yaml, returns its contents (selected_option, additional_context, responded_at), and deletes the file (read-once semantics). If no response file exists, returns {status: no_response}. Use this periodically after escalating to check if a human has provided guidance. Pass your current working directory as workdir to ensure commands execute in the correct project context.

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_swarm_read
description: Read YAML content from a swarm namespace/key. Requires workdir, namespace, and key.

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

name: pm_swarm_write
description: Write validated data into a swarm namespace/key. Requires workdir, namespace, key, and data.

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project

data.description: JSON object payload to validate and persist as YAML

name: pm_swarm_list
description: List keys in a swarm namespace. Requires workdir and namespace.

workdir.description: Working directory containing the target .pm project
